ActionResultRequest.description: ``action.result`` — outcome of an in-world action (schema fixed now, channel E4-5).

action_id.description: Id of the action this reports on.
action_id.title: Action Id

outcome_class.description: Optional machine-readable outcome class such as interrupted or blocked.
outcome_class.title: Outcome Class

ActionResultRequest.title: ActionResultRequest

BridgeError.description: Typed error body carried by a failed response (ADR §2).
BridgeError.title: BridgeError

BridgePingRequest.description: ``bridge.ping`` — connectivity probe for the ADR's ``!bridgePing`` proof.
BridgePingRequest.title: BridgePingRequest

BridgeRequest.description:
Request envelope — Node->Python, or Python->Node for control messages.

Field set is **exactly** ADR §2's request table; ``extra='forbid'`` so an
unknown top-level field is rejected on both sides rather than silently
ignored. ``payload`` is an opaque object here and is validated per-service
via :data:`SERVICE_REGISTRY` / :func:`validate_request`.

agent_id.description: Stable agent identity (e.g. 'vera'); a claim, not proof (ADR §4).
agent_id.title: Agent Id

payload.description: Service-specific body; schema owned per-verb below.
payload.title: Payload

request_id.description: Unique id; correlation + idempotency key for retries (ADR §5).
request_id.title: Request Id

simulation_id.description: Simulation this message belongs to (journal + cost).
simulation_id.title: Simulation Id

trace_id.description: End-to-end correlation id for cross-language tracing (E4-7, #546). Optional and additive (ADR §3 minor bump, protocol 1.1): a 1.0 peer omits it. The server mints one when absent so a request is always traceable in both Node and Python logs by a single id.
trace_id.title: Trace Id

version.description: Protocol semver (ADR §3). Required on every message.
version.title: Version

BridgeRequest.title: BridgeRequest

BridgeResponse.description:
Response envelope — the other direction (ADR §2).

``retryable`` defaults to ``False``: per ADR §5 absence/ambiguity is
treated as *not* retryable, so the default must encode the safe choice.

error.description: Typed error when ok is False.

payload.description: Result body on success; schema owned per-verb below.
payload.title: Payload

retryable.description: Whether the caller may safely retry (ADR §5).
retryable.title: Retryable

trace_id.description: Echoes the request's correlation id (or the one the server minted when the request omitted it) so the same trace id appears on both halves of the round-trip (E4-7, #546). Optional/additive (ADR §3).
trace_id.title: Trace Id

BridgeResponse.title: BridgeResponse

CodeExecuteRequest.description: ``code.execute`` — run code in the existing Docker/gVisor sandbox.

code.description: Source code to run in the sandbox.
code.title: Code

timeout.description: Optional max execution time in seconds; omitted uses the tool default.
timeout.title: Timeout

CodeExecuteRequest.title: CodeExecuteRequest

status.description: Sandbox result state returned by ExecuteCodeTool.
status.title: Status

CostContext.description:
Cost-attribution hints (ADR §2 ``cost_context``).

Carries enough metadata for E11 cost/kill controls to charge the right
account: which model tier the call is for and which budget bucket it draws
from. ``estimated_cost_usd`` is an optional pre-flight hint the cost gate
may use; the authoritative number is computed Python-side.

agent_tier.description: Which model tier this call bills against (drives cost attribution).
agent_tier.title: Agent Tier

budget_bucket.description: Budget account this draws from, e.g. 'daily-global' or 'weekly-vera'.
budget_bucket.title: Budget Bucket

CostContext.title: CostContext

CostGateRequest.description: ``cost.gate`` — check whether a spend/action is allowed (ADR §6).
CostGateRequest.title: CostGateRequest

remaining_budget_usd.description: Budget left in the relevant bucket after this decision.
remaining_budget_usd.title: Remaining Budget Usd

DirectorBuildMacro.description: Director-scheduled build macro context for one prompt verdict.

granted.description: Whether the agent may invoke !planAndBuild for this verdict.
granted.title: Granted

owner.description: Agent that owns the build plan.
owner.title: Owner

role.description: Whether this agent owns the plan or supports it.
role.title: Role

scene_id.description: Scene id for this build macro decision.
scene_id.title: Scene Id

support_task.description: Short support instruction for non-owner agents.
support_task.title: Support Task

DirectorBuildMacro.title: DirectorBuildMacro

DirectorGateRequest.description: ``director.gate`` — Director V2 prompt eligibility check for Mindcraft.

active_objective.description: Current settlement phase objective when a multi-phase build is active.

available_tools.description: Mindcraft action/tool affordances available to the selected prompt.
available_tools.title: Available Tools

event_kind.description: Kind of scene event that would trigger a Mindcraft prompt.
event_kind.title: Event Kind

mentions.description: Directly addressed agent ids parsed by the caller.
mentions.title: Mentions

plan_build_agent_allowlist.description: Run-scoped agent ids allowed to own !planAndBuild phases.
plan_build_agent_allowlist.title: Plan Build Agent Allowlist

position.description: Best-known local Minecraft position for spatial scheduling.

scene_hint.description: Optional caller-side grouping hint for batched messages.
scene_hint.title: Scene Hint

source_agent.description: Agent or speaker that caused the event, when known.
source_agent.title: Source Agent

DirectorGateRequest.title: DirectorGateRequest

granted_tools.description: Action/tool affordances granted to this selected prompt.
granted_tools.title: Granted Tools

local_observations.description: Local scene observations to include in selected prompt context.
local_observations.title: Local Observations

role.description: Role assigned to the selected prompt context.
role.title: Role

scene_digest.description: Compact scene context for the selected prompt.
scene_digest.title: Scene Digest

scene_id.description: Director V2 scene id used for the decision.
scene_id.title: Scene Id

selected.description: Whether this bot may enter the prompt path.
selected.title: Selected

suppressed_agents.description: Known agents suppressed by this scene decision.
suppressed_agents.title: Suppressed Agents

suppression_reason.description: Why this bot was suppressed, when selected is false.
suppression_reason.title: Suppression Reason

turn_kind.description: Selected turn class, or null when suppressed/bypassed.
turn_kind.title: Turn Kind

ErrandCompleteRequest.description:
``errand.complete`` — Alpha reports a verified errand outcome.

E7-3 uses this verb to connect the embodied action result back to the
dispatcher: ``symbol`` is Alpha's non-verbal ✓/✗/? summary, while
``step_results`` preserves the per-action verified outcome.

step_results.description: Verified terminal results for each errand step.
step_results.title: Step Results

symbol.description: Alpha's non-verbal outcome symbol: success, failure, or confused.
symbol.title: Symbol

ErrandCompleteRequest.title: ErrandCompleteRequest

accepted.description: Whether Python recorded the errand completion.
accepted.title: Accepted

ErrandPollRequest.description: ``errand.poll`` — bot asks Python for its next dispatched task.

agent_id.description: Stable id of the polling agent.
agent_id.title: Agent Id

ErrandPollRequest.title: ErrandPollRequest

dispatched_at_ms.description: Unix epoch milliseconds when the task was queued.
dispatched_at_ms.title: Dispatched At Ms

urgency.description: Dispatch urgency, or null when no task is pending.
urgency.title: Urgency

ErrandStepResult.description: One terminal in-world action outcome within an Alpha errand.

action_id.description: Step action id from the errand plan.
action_id.title: Action Id

status.description: Verified terminal status reported by the action surface.
status.title: Status

ErrandStepResult.title: ErrandStepResult

equipment.description: Equipment slot name -> normalized item id or null.
equipment.title: Equipment

items.description: Inventory stacks included in this snapshot.
items.title: Items

KillStatusRequest.description: ``kill.status`` — query the global operator kill switch.
KillStatusRequest.title: KillStatusRequest

active.description: Whether the global kill switch is active.
active.title: Active

reason.description: Stable reason string when active or when status had to fail safe.
reason.title: Reason

ttl_seconds.description: Redis TTL for the kill switch key, or null when absent/unknown.
ttl_seconds.title: Ttl Seconds

agent_id.description: Agent whose output is under review.
agent_id.title: Agent Id

context.description: Optional surrounding context for the filter.
context.title: Context

sanitized_text.description: Cleaned text when the filter rewrote rather than vetoed.
sanitized_text.title: Sanitized Text

tier.description: Memory tier to read. 'recall' performs semantic recall; 'core' fetches the agent's Tier 1 core memory. Query remains required for additive compatibility and is ignored for tier='core'.
tier.title: Tier

core_memory.description: Core memory markdown returned by the core memory manager.
core_memory.title: Core Memory

formatted.description: Formatted markdown returned by the recall memory manager.
formatted.title: Formatted

memory_id.description: Stable id of the recalled memory.
memory_id.title: Memory Id

MemoryWriteRequest.description: ``memory.write`` — persist a memory. Idempotent on the request_id (ADR §5).
MemoryWriteRequest.title: MemoryWriteRequest

memory_id.description: Id of the stored (or de-duplicated) memory.
memory_id.title: Memory Id

PerceptionReportRequest.description: ``perception.report`` — bot-observed world state (schema fixed now, channel E4-6).

observations.description: Structured world observations from the bot.
observations.title: Observations

PerceptionReportRequest.title: PerceptionReportRequest

PerceptionSnapshot.description: Stable perception snapshot for E6 embodied decisions.
PerceptionSnapshot.title: PerceptionSnapshot

PoseObservation.description: Bot pose at the instant a perception snapshot was captured.

on_ground.description: Whether the bot is on the ground.
on_ground.title: On Ground

PoseObservation.title: PoseObservation

RescueTaskRequest.description: Structured rescue request carried in shared-state tasks and bot commands.
RescueTaskRequest.title: RescueTaskRequest

RescueTaskResponse.description: Bridge-friendly rescue outcome shape used by action.result details.
RescueTaskResponse.title: RescueTaskResponse

SharedStateWriteRequest.description: ``shared_state.write`` — advisory update to the embodied blackboard.
SharedStateWriteRequest.title: SharedStateWriteRequest

Vec3.description: 3D coordinate in world space or a block cell.
Vec3.title: Vec3
